content: Alcohol use disorder (AUD) is a chronic relapsing brain disease characterized by compulsive alcohol use, loss of control over intake, and negative emotional state when not using. DSM-5 criteria include 11 symptoms ranging from mild (2-3 symptoms) to severe (6+ symptoms): drinking more/longer than intended, unsuccessful cut-down attempts, time spent obtaining/using/recovering, cravings, failure to fulfill obligations, social/interpersonal problems, giving up activities, hazardous use, continued use despite physical/psychological problems, tolerance, and withdrawal. Alcohol causes over 200 health conditions including liver disease, pancreatitis, cardiovascular disease, multiple cancers, and neurological damage. Global burden: 3 million deaths annually (5.3% of all deaths). Binge drinking (4+ drinks women, 5+ men in 2 hours) poses acute risks even without AUD diagnosis.
